package repository

import (
	"net/http"

	"github.com/gin-gonic/gin"
	"gorm.io/gorm"
)

// Anti-pattern: Repository package imports gin and net/http types.
// The data layer should not depend on HTTP transport packages.

type Account struct {
	ID      string `gorm:"column:id"`
	Balance int    `gorm:"column:balance"`
}

type AccountRepository struct {
	db *gorm.DB
}

// Find returns gin.H - a gin-specific type - from the repository layer.
func (r *AccountRepository) Find(id string) (gin.H, error) {
	var account Account
	err := r.db.First(&account, "id = ?", id).Error
	if err != nil {
		return gin.H{"error": "not found", "status": http.StatusNotFound}, err
	}
	return gin.H{"id": account.ID, "balance": account.Balance}, nil
}

// Create uses http.ResponseWriter directly - another transport dependency.
func (r *AccountRepository) Create(w http.ResponseWriter, account *Account) error {
	result := r.db.Create(account)
	if result.Error != nil {
		w.WriteHeader(http.StatusInternalServerError)
		return result.Error
	}
	w.WriteHeader(http.StatusCreated)
	return nil
}

// StatusCheck returns an http.Status code from the repository.
func (r *AccountRepository) StatusCheck(id string) int {
	var account Account
	if err := r.db.First(&account, "id = ?", id).Error; err != nil {
		return http.StatusNotFound
	}
	return http.StatusOK
}
